rows.notes: Combined 'II (Capital)' and '1893.' into a single header description for logical grouping, aligning with gemini-flash's approach for this header section. gpt-5-mini split these into two separate rows. Visual inspection confirms these are distinct elements of the overall page title/header. No monetary values are associated with this row.

Visual verification (Step 5) clearly confirms the presence of monetary amounts (13 pounds, 4 shillings, 6 pence) directly associated with this descriptive row. gemini-flash missed these amounts entirely, classifying it as a pure 'header'. gpt-5-mini correctly identified these amounts and the 'entry' row_type. The description punctuation was adjusted from gpt-5-mini's 'Fund.' to 'Fund' based on visual inspection.
rows.english_description: Capital held in the Local Rates Composition Fund (a fund set aside relating to local rates).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Capital held in the Local Rates Composition Fund, amounting to £13 4s 6d.

rows.notes: Both extractors agreed on the monetary amounts (3888, 13, 3), which are clearly legible. Punctuation for the description was chosen as '.' (from gemini-flash) over ':' (from gpt-5-mini) based on visual inspection. For row_type, gemini-flash identified it as 'entry' and gpt-5-mini as 'total'. Per standard ledger practices, 'Balance at close of Acct.' functions as an itemized entry (e.g., a balance brought or carried forward) rather than the ultimate final sum marker, which is the subsequent row. Therefore, 'entry' was selected.
rows.english_description: Balance at the close of the capital account.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Balance at the close of the capital account, amounting to £3888 13s 3d.

rows.notes: The monetary amounts (3901, 17, 9) and 'total' row_type were in consensus. The description for the pound symbol varied ('£' from gemini-flash vs. 'L' from gpt-5-mini); '£' was chosen as the standard representation for the stylized symbol. gpt-5-mini's note provided excellent reasoning by performing arithmetic validation, confirming that this total (3901.17.9) is the exact sum of the two preceding entries (13.4.6 + 3888.13.3). This significantly increased confidence in the accuracy of all three related amount rows.
